questions.questionText: A direct current of 6 A is superimposed on an alternating current I = 10 sin $$\omega$$t flowing through a wire. The effective value of the resulting current will be
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, $$I = 6 + 10\sin \omega t$$

$${I_{eft}} = \left[ {{{\int\limits_0^T {{I^2}dt} } \over {\int\limits_0^T {dt} }}} \right] = {\left[ {{1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {{{(6 + 10\sin \omega t)}^2}dt} } \right]^{1/2}}$$

$$ = {\left[ {{1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {(36 + 120\sin \omega t + 100{{\sin }^2}\omega t)dt} } \right]^{1/2}}$$

But as, $${1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {\sin \omega t\,dt = 0} $$ and $${1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {{{\sin }^2}\omega t = {1 \over 2}} $$

$$ \Rightarrow {I_{eff}} = {\left[ {36 + {1 \over 2} \times 100} \right]^{1/2}} = 9.27$$

Thus, $${I_{eff}} = 9.27\,A$$

questions.questionText: In the circuit shown in the figure, the AC source gives a voltage V = 20 cos (2000t) neglecting source resistance, the voltmeter and ammeter reading will be
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, R1 = 8 $$\Omega$$

R2 = 2 $$\Omega$$

L = 5 mH

C = 50 $$\mu$$F

and V0 = 20 cos (2000t)

Impedance, $$Z = \sqrt {{R^2} + {{({X_L} - {X_C})}^2}} $$

As, here, $${X_L} = \omega L = 2000 \times 5 \times {10^{ - 3}} = 10\Omega $$

Similarly, $${X_L} = {1 \over {\omega C}} = {1 \over {2000 \times 50 \times {{10}^{ - 6}}}} = 10\Omega $$

$$\because$$ $${X_L} = {X_C}$$, Hence, $${i_{\max }} = {{{V_0}} \over Z} = {{20} \over {(8 + 2)}} = 2A$$

Hence, $${i_{rms}} = {{{i_{\max }}} \over {\sqrt 2 }} = {2 \over {\sqrt 2 }} = 1.41 \,A$$

and $$V = {R_2}\,{i_{rms}} = 1.41 \times 2 = 2.82\,V$$

questions.questionText: In a radioactive material the activity at time t1, is A1 and at a later time t2, it is A2. If the decay constant of the material is $$\lambda$$, then
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
From radioactive decay law,

$$ - {{dN} \over {dt}} \propto N$$ or $$ - {{dN} \over {dt}} = \lambda N$$

Thus, $$A = - {{dN} \over {dt}}$$ or $$A = \lambda N$$

$$ \Rightarrow A = \lambda {N_0}{e^{ - \lambda t}}$$ ...... (i)

Where, $${A_0} = \lambda {N_0}$$ is the activity of the radioactive material at time, t = 0

At time, $${A_1} = {A_0}\,{e^{ - \lambda {t_1}}}$$ ..... (ii)

At time, $${A_2} = {A_0}\,{e^{ - \lambda {t_2}}}$$ ..... (iii)

On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (iii), we have

$${{{A_1}} \over {{A_2}}} = {{{e^{ - \lambda {t_1}}}} \over {{e^{ - \lambda {t_2}}}}} = {e^{ - \lambda ({t_1} - {t_2})}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {A_1} = {A_2}\,{e^{ - \lambda ({t_1} - {t_2})}}$$

questions.questionText: A particle of mass m is projected with velocity v at an angle $$\theta$$ with the horizontal. At its highest point, it explodes into two pieces of equal mass, one of the piece continue to move on the original trajectory, then the velocity of second piece is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
According to the law of conservation of linear momentum at the highest point,

$$mv\cos \theta = {m \over 2}(v\cos \theta ) + {m \over 2}v'$$

$$ \Rightarrow mv\cos \theta - {m \over 2}v\cos \theta = {m \over 2}v'$$

$$ \Rightarrow v' = 2\left( {v\cos \theta - {{v\cos \theta } \over 2}} \right) = v\cos \theta $$

questions.questionText: In the arrangement shown in figure, when the switch S2 is open, the galvanometer, shows no deflection for $$l$$ = 50 cm when the switch S2 is closed, the galvanometer shows no deflection for $$l$$ = 0.416 m. The internal resistance (r) of 6 V cell is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
When S2 open.

Assume resistance of XY = R.

Resistance of wire per unit length, x = $$\frac{R}{L}$$ = R $$\Omega$$ m$$-$$1

$$\because$$ I = E0 / R

Now, the potential drop across 50 cm length is 6 V, so

$$\frac{E_0}{R}$$ $$\times$$ R $$\times$$ $$\frac{50}{100}=6$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ E0 = 12 V

When S2 closed, potential drop across 0.416 cm length,

V1 = $$\frac{E_0}{R}\times$$ R $$\times\,0.416=12\times0.416\approx 5$$ V

Hence, E $$-$$ Ir = 5V

$$\Rightarrow$$ 6 $$-$$ Ir = 5

$$\because$$ I = $$\frac{5}{10}$$

$$\therefore$$ 6 $$-$$ = $$\frac{5}{10}$$r

$$\Rightarrow$$ r = 2$$\Omega$$

questions.questionText: The stopping potential (V0) versus frequency $$\nu $$ of a graph for photoelectric effect in a metal. From the graph, the Plank's constant (h) is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$e{V_0} = h( u - { u _0}) = h u - h{ u _0}$$

$${V_0} = \left( {{h \over e}} ight) u - \left( {{h \over e}} ight){ u _0}$$ ..... (i)

From Eq. (i), it follows that the slope of the graph is $${h \over e}$$.

Therefore,

h = e $$ imes$$ slope = 1.6 $$ imes$$ 10$$-$$19 $$ imes$$ $${{16.5 - 0} \over {(8 - 4) imes {{10}^{15}}}}$$

$$ = {{1.6 imes {{10}^{ - 19}} imes 16.5} \over {4 imes {{10}^{ - 5}}}} = 6.6 imes {10^{ - 34}}$$ J-s.

questions.questionText: The de-Broglie wavelength of an electron moving with a velocity $$\frac{c}{3}$$ (c = 3 $$\times$$ 108 m/s) is equal to the wavelength of photon. The ratio of the kinetic energies of electron and photon is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
The de-Broglie wavelength, $$\lambda = {h \over {mv}}$$

Here, $${\lambda _e} = {h \over {{m_e}{e \over 3}}}$$ and $${\lambda _p} = {h \over {{m_p}c}}$$

Given, $${\lambda _e} = {\lambda _p}$$

So, $${h \over {{m_e}{c \over 3}}} = {h \over {{m_p}c}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{m_e}} \over {{m_p}}} = 3$$

Ratio of $$KE = {{{K_e}} \over {{K_p}}} = {{{1 \over 2}{m_e}v_e^2} \over {{1 \over 2}{m_p}v_p^2}} = {1 \over 3}$$

($$\because$$ $${{{m_e}} \over {{m_p}}} = 3,\,{v_e} = {c \over 3}$$ and $${v_p} = c$$)

questions.questionText: An electromagnetic wave is propagating along X-axis. At x = 1 cm and t = 18s, its electric vector |E| = 8 V/m, then the magnitude of its magnetic vector is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
As we know that, $${E \over B} = c$$

so, $$B = {E \over c} = {8 \over {3 \times {{10}^8}}} = 2.66 \times {10^{ - 8}}$$

questions.questionText: A direct current of 6 A is superimposed on an alternating current I = 10 sin $$\omega$$t flowing through a wire. The effective value of the resulting current will be
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, $$I = 6 + 10\sin \omega t$$

$${I_{eft}} = \left[ {{{\int\limits_0^T {{I^2}dt} } \over {\int\limits_0^T {dt} }}} \right] = {\left[ {{1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {{{(6 + 10\sin \omega t)}^2}dt} } \right]^{1/2}}$$

$$ = {\left[ {{1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {(36 + 120\sin \omega t + 100{{\sin }^2}\omega t)dt} } \right]^{1/2}}$$

But as, $${1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {\sin \omega t\,dt = 0} $$ and $${1 \over T}\int\limits_0^T {{{\sin }^2}\omega t = {1 \over 2}} $$

$$ \Rightarrow {I_{eff}} = {\left[ {36 + {1 \over 2} \times 100} \right]^{1/2}} = 9.27$$

Thus, $${I_{eff}} = 9.27\,A$$

questions.questionText: In the circuit shown in the figure, the AC source gives a voltage V = 20 cos (2000t) neglecting source resistance, the voltmeter and ammeter reading will be
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, R1 = 8 $$\Omega$$

R2 = 2 $$\Omega$$

L = 5 mH

C = 50 $$\mu$$F

and V0 = 20 cos (2000t)

Impedance, $$Z = \sqrt {{R^2} + {{({X_L} - {X_C})}^2}} $$

As, here, $${X_L} = \omega L = 2000 \times 5 \times {10^{ - 3}} = 10\Omega $$

Similarly, $${X_L} = {1 \over {\omega C}} = {1 \over {2000 \times 50 \times {{10}^{ - 6}}}} = 10\Omega $$

$$\because$$ $${X_L} = {X_C}$$, Hence, $${i_{\max }} = {{{V_0}} \over Z} = {{20} \over {(8 + 2)}} = 2A$$

Hence, $${i_{rms}} = {{{i_{\max }}} \over {\sqrt 2 }} = {2 \over {\sqrt 2 }} = 1.41 \,A$$

and $$V = {R_2}\,{i_{rms}} = 1.41 \times 2 = 2.82\,V$$

questions.questionText: In a radioactive material the activity at time t1, is A1 and at a later time t2, it is A2. If the decay constant of the material is $$\lambda$$, then
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
From radioactive decay law,

$$ - {{dN} \over {dt}} \propto N$$ or $$ - {{dN} \over {dt}} = \lambda N$$

Thus, $$A = - {{dN} \over {dt}}$$ or $$A = \lambda N$$

$$ \Rightarrow A = \lambda {N_0}{e^{ - \lambda t}}$$ ...... (i)

Where, $${A_0} = \lambda {N_0}$$ is the activity of the radioactive material at time, t = 0

At time, $${A_1} = {A_0}\,{e^{ - \lambda {t_1}}}$$ ..... (ii)

At time, $${A_2} = {A_0}\,{e^{ - \lambda {t_2}}}$$ ..... (iii)

On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (iii), we have

$${{{A_1}} \over {{A_2}}} = {{{e^{ - \lambda {t_1}}}} \over {{e^{ - \lambda {t_2}}}}} = {e^{ - \lambda ({t_1} - {t_2})}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {A_1} = {A_2}\,{e^{ - \lambda ({t_1} - {t_2})}}$$

According to the law of conservation of linear momentum at the highest point,

questions.questionText: Which one of the following graphs represents the variation of electric potential with distance r from the centre of a non-conducting charged sphere of radius R?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Electric potential at centre R = 0

Vcentre = 1.5 Vsurface

where,

$${V_0} = {{kQ} \over R}$$

$${V_{inside}} = {{kQ} \over {2R}}\left( {3 - {{{r^2}} \over {{R^2}}}} \right)$$

$${V_{outside}} = {{kQ} \over r} \propto {1 \over r}$$

questions.questionText: A capillary tube is attached horizontally to a constant heat arrangement. If the radius of the capillary tube is increased by 25%, then the rate of flow of liquid will change nearly by
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Liquid volume coming out of the tube per second

$$V = {{p\pi {r^4}} \over {8\eta l}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow V\,\alpha {r^4}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{V_2}} \over {{V_1}}} = {\left[ {{{{r_2}} \over {{r_1}}}} \right]^4} \Rightarrow {V_2} = {V_1}{\left[ {{{125} \over {100}}} \right]^4} = {V_1}{\left( {{5 \over 4}} \right)^4}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {V_2} = {V_1}(244)$$

So, now $${{\Delta V} \over V} = {{{V_2} - {V_1}} \over {{V_1}}} = {{(244){V_1} - {V_1}} \over {{V_1}}} = 144\% $$

questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements in relation to the hydrogen atom is correct?
options: 3s, 3p and 3d-orbitals all have the same energy.
3s and 3p-orbitals are of lower energy than 3d-orbital.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: The hydrogen atom has the configuration 1s and 3s, 3p and 3d-orbitals will have same energy with respect to 1s-orbital.

questions.questionText: Glucose reacts with X number of molecules of phenyl hydrazine to yield osazone. The value of X is,
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Hence, three molecules of phenyl hydrazine (C6H5NHNH2) is used to yield osazone.

questions.questionText: OF and F2 can be compared in terms of
options: OF is paramagnetic while F<sub>2</sub> is diamagnetic
OF is more stable towards dissociation into atoms
None of the above is correct
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
This is one unpaired electron in (O$$-$$F), hence, paramagnetic, thus (a) is correct.

As bond order of O$$-$$F > F$$-$$F thus, bond energy of O$$-$$F > F$$-$$F.

Hence, (a) and (b) both are correct.

questions.questionText: The structure of H2O2 is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) is a non-planar molecule with twisted symmetrical structure. It has non-linear structure with an open book structure.

questions.questionText:
In which pair or pairs is the stronger bond found in the first species?

I. O$$_2^{2 - }$$, O2; II. N2, N$$_2^{+ }$$; III. NO+, NO$$-$$
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Larger the bond order, stronger the bond formed.

questions.questionText: In the molecules CH4, NF3, NH$$_4^ + $$ and H2O
options: number of lone pairs are same
all have same hybridisation of centre of atom
number of bond pairs are same
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
CH4 ; bp = 4; sp3-hybridisation

NF3 ; bp = 3; $$l$$p = 1; sp3-hybridisation

NH$$_4^ + $$ ; bp = 4; $$lp$$ = 0; sp3-hybridisation

H2O; bp = 2; lp = 2; sp3-hybridisation

Hence, all have the same slope.

questions.questionText: What is [NH$$_{4}^+$$] in a solution that is 0.02 M NH3 0.01 M KOH ? [Kb(NH3) = 1.8 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For an isomerisation reaction A $$\rightleftharpoons$$ B, the temperature dependence of equilibrium constant is given by

loge K = 4.0 $$-$$ $$\frac{2000}{T}$$

The value of $$\Delta$$S$$^\circ$$ at Hook is, therefore
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
van't Hoff equation giving variation of equilibrium constant, K with temperature T is

loge K = $$-$$ $$\frac{\Delta H^\circ}{RT}+\frac{\Delta S^\circ}{R}$$

Given, loge K = $$-$$ $$\frac{2000}{T}+4.0$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\Delta$$S$$^\circ$$/R = 4 $$\Rightarrow$$ $$\Delta$$S$$^\circ$$ = 4R

questions.questionText: Ninhydrin gives yellow colour in paper chromatography with which amino acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Option B : Proline

Explanation :

Ninhydrin is a chemical reagent commonly used to detect amino acids, peptides, and proteins. While most amino acids react with ninhydrin to give a purple-blue color, Proline and hydroxyproline are exceptions. These two amino acids react with ninhydrin to give a yellow color instead. This is because proline is a secondary amino acid, meaning its amino group is a part of a ring structure, which alters its reactivity compared to primary amino acids.

questions.questionText: What is the hybridisation of [CrF6]3$$-$$ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The electronic configuration of Cr is [Ar] 3d5 4s1.

[CrF6]3$$-$$ has d2sp3-hybridisation and octahedral geometry.

questions.questionText: A running track of 440 ft is to be laid out enclosing a football field, the shape of which is a rectangle with a semi-circle at each end. If the area of the rectangular portion is to be maximum, then the lengths of its side are
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Perimeter = 440 ft

$$\Rightarrow$$ 2x + $$\pi$$r + $$\pi$$r = 440

2x + 2$$\pi$$r = 440

A = Area of the rectangular portion = x2r

A = $$x\frac{(440-2x)}{\pi}=\frac{1}{\pi}(440x-2x^2)$$

Let $$\frac{dA}{dx}=\frac{1}{\pi}(440-4x)=0$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ x = 110 for which $$\frac{d^2A}{dx^2} < 0$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ A is maximum when x = 110

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$2r=\frac{440-2x}{\pi}=\frac{440-220}{22/7}=70$$

$$\therefore$$ r = 35 ft and x = 110 ft

questions.questionText: A spherical balloon is filled with 4500$$\pi$$ cubic meters of helium gas. If a leak in the balloon causes the gas to escape at the rate of 72$$\pi$$ cubic meters per minute then the rate (in meters per minute) at which the radius of the balloon decreases 49 min after the leakage began is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
V = $$\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3\Rightarrow4500\pi=\frac{4\pi r^3}{3}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ r = 15 m

After 49 min = (4500 $$-$$ 49.72) $$\pi$$

= 972 $$\pi$$m3

$$\Rightarrow$$ 972$$\pi=\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ r3 = 3 $$\times$$ 243 = 3 $$\times$$ 35

r = 9

$$\frac{dv}{dt}=4 \pi r^2\left(\frac{dr}{dt}\right)$$

72$$\pi$$ = 4$$\pi$$ $$\times$$ 9 $$\times$$ $$\left(\frac{dr}{dt}\right)$$

$$\frac{dr}{dt}=\left(\frac{2}{9}\right)$$

questions.questionText: What is the area enclosed by the parabola described by $${(y - 2)^2} = (x - 1)$$, its tangent line at the point (2, 3), and the X-axis?
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
The equation of the parabola $${y^2} - 4y - x + 5 = 0$$

the equation of tangent at (2, 3) is

$$3y - 2(y + 3) - {{x + 2} \over 2} + 5 = 0$$

$$2y - x - 4 = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ Required area A is given by

$$A = \int_0^3 {({x_2} - {x_1})dy} $$

$$ \Rightarrow A = \int_0^3 {[\{ {{(y - 2)}^3} + 1\} - 2(2y - 4\} ]dy} $$

$$ \Rightarrow A = \int_0^3 {({y^2} - 6y + 9)dy = \int_0^3 {{{(3 - y)}^2}dy} } $$

$$ = - \left[ {{{{{(3 - y)}^3}} \over 3}} \right]_0^3 = 9$$

questions.questionText: The area enclosed by the curves $$y = \sin x + \cos x$$ and $$y = |\cos x - \sin x|$$ over the interval $$\left[ {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right]$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given, $$y = \sin x + \cos x,\,\,x \in [0,\pi /2]$$

$${{dy} \over {dx}} = \cos x - \sin x$$

$$y = |\cos x - \sin x|$$

$$ = \left[ {\matrix{ {\cos x - \sin x} & {x \in [0,\pi /4]} \cr {\sin x - \cos x} & {x \in [\pi /4,\pi /2]} \cr } } \right]$$

$$\therefore$$ Required area

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {|(\sin x + \cos x) - (\cos x - \sin x)|dx + \int_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} {|2\cos x|dx} } $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {|2\sin x|dx + \int_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} {|2\cos x|dx} } $$

$$ = 2( - \cos x)_0^{\pi /4} + 2(\sin x)_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2}$$

$$ = 2\left[ { - {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }} + 1 + 1 - {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right]$$

$$ = 2\left( {2 - {2 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right)$$

$$ = 2(2 - \sqrt 2 )$$

$$ = 4 - 2\sqrt 2 $$

$$ = 2\sqrt 2 (\sqrt 2 - 1)$$

questions.questionText: The number of terms in the expansion of $${(1 + 5\sqrt {2x} )^9} + {(1 - 5\sqrt {2x} )^9}$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (x + y)n is 1024, then the value of the greatest coefficient in the expansion is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given, sum of the coefficient = 1024

i.e. 2n = 1024 = 210

$$\Rightarrow$$ n = 10

Since, n is even, so greatest coefficient

= nCn/2 = 10C5 = 252

questions.questionText: The locus of the mid-point of the chord if contact of tangents drawn from points lying on the straight line $$4x - 5y = 20$$ to the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = 9$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $$P\left( {t,{{4t - 20} \over 5}} \right)$$ be a point on the line $$4x - 5y = 20$$. Then the chord of contact of tangents drawn from P to the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = 9$$ is

$$tx + \left( {{{4t - 20} \over 5}} \right)y = 9$$ ...... (i)

Let $$\theta$$ (h, k) be the mid-point of this chord of contact, then its equation is also $$hx + ky = {h^2} + {k^2}$$ ..... (ii) [using $$T = S'$$]

Clearly Eqs. (i) and (ii) represent the same line

$$\therefore$$ $${t \over h} = {{4t - 20} \over {5k}} = {9 \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {t \over h} = {9 \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$ and $${t \over h} = {{4t - 20} \over {5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow t = {{9h} \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$ and $$t = {{20h} \over {4h - 5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{9h} \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}} = {{20h} \over {4h - 5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow h\{ 20({h^2} + {k^2}) - 36h + 45k\} = 0$$

$$x = 0$$ or $$[20({x^2} + {y^2}) - 36x + 45y] = 0$$

questions.questionText: If $$|w| = 2$$, then the set of points $$z = w - {1 \over w}$$ is contained in or equal to the set of points z satisfying
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
To solve this, we need to first express $$w$$ in the polar form since we know the modulus (or absolute value) of $$w$$.

$$w = |w|e^{iθ} = 2e^{iθ}$$

where $$θ$$ is the argument of $$w$$.

Substitute this into the expression for $$z$$:

$$z = w - {1 \over w} = 2e^{iθ} - {1 \over 2e^{iθ}}$$

By applying Euler's formula, which states that $$e^{iθ} = cos(θ) + isin(θ)$$, the expression becomes :

$$z = 2[cos(θ) + isin(θ)] - {1 \over 2}[cos(θ) - isin(θ)]$$

Simplify the expression :

$$z = [2cos(θ) - {1 \over 2}cos(θ)] + i[2sin(θ) + {1 \over 2}sin(θ)]$$

$$z = {3 \over 2}cos(θ) + {5 \over 2}isin(θ)$$

The modulus of $z$ can be found using the formula

$|z| = \sqrt{(Re(z))^2 + (Im(z))^2}$, where $Re(z)$ and $Im(z)$ are the real and imaginary parts of $z$, respectively.

So,

$|z| = \sqrt{(\frac{3}{2} \cos(θ))^2 + (\frac{5}{2} \sin(θ))^2} = \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} \cos^2(θ)) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

We know that $\sin^2(θ) + \cos^2(θ) = 1$ (based on the Pythagorean trigonometric identity), so let's replace $\cos^2(θ)$ with $1 - \sin^2(θ)$ in the equation above.

$|z| = \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} (1 - \sin^2(θ))) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

$= \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} - \frac{9}{4} \sin^2(θ)) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

$= \sqrt{\frac{9}{4} + \frac{16}{4} \sin^2(θ)}$

$= \sqrt{\frac{9 + 16 \sin^2(θ)}{4}}$

$ = \frac{1}{2} \sqrt{9 + 16 \sin^2(θ)}$

Now, $\sin^2(θ)$ is always less than or equal to 1, so $16 \sin^2(θ)$ is always less than or equal to 16.

So, the maximum value of $|z|$ is when $\sin^2(θ) = 1$, which makes $|z|$ equal to $\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{9 + 16} = \frac{1}{2} \sqrt{25} = \frac{5}{2}$.

So the set of points $z$ satisfying $|z| ≤ 3$ (Option D) includes all points $z$, and thus it is the correct choice.

questions.questionText: A running track of 440 ft is to be laid out enclosing a football field, the shape of which is a rectangle with a semi-circle at each end. If the area of the rectangular portion is to be maximum, then the lengths of its side are
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Perimeter = 440 ft

$$\Rightarrow$$ 2x + $$\pi$$r + $$\pi$$r = 440

2x + 2$$\pi$$r = 440

A = Area of the rectangular portion = x2r

A = $$x\frac{(440-2x)}{\pi}=\frac{1}{\pi}(440x-2x^2)$$

Let $$\frac{dA}{dx}=\frac{1}{\pi}(440-4x)=0$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ x = 110 for which $$\frac{d^2A}{dx^2} < 0$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ A is maximum when x = 110

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$2r=\frac{440-2x}{\pi}=\frac{440-220}{22/7}=70$$

$$\therefore$$ r = 35 ft and x = 110 ft

questions.questionText: A spherical balloon is filled with 4500$$\pi$$ cubic meters of helium gas. If a leak in the balloon causes the gas to escape at the rate of 72$$\pi$$ cubic meters per minute then the rate (in meters per minute) at which the radius of the balloon decreases 49 min after the leakage began is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
V = $$\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3\Rightarrow4500\pi=\frac{4\pi r^3}{3}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ r = 15 m

After 49 min = (4500 $$-$$ 49.72) $$\pi$$

= 972 $$\pi$$m3

$$\Rightarrow$$ 972$$\pi=\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ r3 = 3 $$\times$$ 243 = 3 $$\times$$ 35

r = 9

$$\frac{dv}{dt}=4 \pi r^2\left(\frac{dr}{dt}\right)$$

72$$\pi$$ = 4$$\pi$$ $$\times$$ 9 $$\times$$ $$\left(\frac{dr}{dt}\right)$$

$$\frac{dr}{dt}=\left(\frac{2}{9}\right)$$

questions.questionText: What is the area enclosed by the parabola described by $${(y - 2)^2} = (x - 1)$$, its tangent line at the point (2, 3), and the X-axis?
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
The equation of the parabola $${y^2} - 4y - x + 5 = 0$$

the equation of tangent at (2, 3) is

$$3y - 2(y + 3) - {{x + 2} \over 2} + 5 = 0$$

$$2y - x - 4 = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ Required area A is given by

$$A = \int_0^3 {({x_2} - {x_1})dy} $$

$$ \Rightarrow A = \int_0^3 {[\{ {{(y - 2)}^3} + 1\} - 2(2y - 4\} ]dy} $$

$$ \Rightarrow A = \int_0^3 {({y^2} - 6y + 9)dy = \int_0^3 {{{(3 - y)}^2}dy} } $$

$$ = - \left[ {{{{{(3 - y)}^3}} \over 3}} \right]_0^3 = 9$$

questions.questionText: The area enclosed by the curves $$y = \sin x + \cos x$$ and $$y = |\cos x - \sin x|$$ over the interval $$\left[ {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right]$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given, $$y = \sin x + \cos x,\,\,x \in [0,\pi /2]$$

$${{dy} \over {dx}} = \cos x - \sin x$$

$$y = |\cos x - \sin x|$$

$$ = \left[ {\matrix{ {\cos x - \sin x} & {x \in [0,\pi /4]} \cr {\sin x - \cos x} & {x \in [\pi /4,\pi /2]} \cr } } \right]$$

$$\therefore$$ Required area

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {|(\sin x + \cos x) - (\cos x - \sin x)|dx + \int_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} {|2\cos x|dx} } $$

$$ = \int_0^{\pi /4} {|2\sin x|dx + \int_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2} {|2\cos x|dx} } $$

$$ = 2( - \cos x)_0^{\pi /4} + 2(\sin x)_{\pi /4}^{\pi /2}$$

$$ = 2\left[ { - {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }} + 1 + 1 - {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right]$$

$$ = 2\left( {2 - {2 \over {\sqrt 2 }}} \right)$$

$$ = 2(2 - \sqrt 2 )$$

$$ = 4 - 2\sqrt 2 $$

$$ = 2\sqrt 2 (\sqrt 2 - 1)$$

questions.questionText: The number of terms in the expansion of $${(1 + 5\sqrt {2x} )^9} + {(1 - 5\sqrt {2x} )^9}$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given, sum of the coefficient = 1024

i.e. 2n = 1024 = 210

$$\Rightarrow$$ n = 10

Since, n is even, so greatest coefficient

= nCn/2 = 10C5 = 252

questions.questionText: The locus of the mid-point of the chord if contact of tangents drawn from points lying on the straight line $$4x - 5y = 20$$ to the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = 9$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $$P\left( {t,{{4t - 20} \over 5}} \right)$$ be a point on the line $$4x - 5y = 20$$. Then the chord of contact of tangents drawn from P to the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = 9$$ is

$$tx + \left( {{{4t - 20} \over 5}} \right)y = 9$$ ...... (i)

Let $$\theta$$ (h, k) be the mid-point of this chord of contact, then its equation is also $$hx + ky = {h^2} + {k^2}$$ ..... (ii) [using $$T = S'$$]

Clearly Eqs. (i) and (ii) represent the same line

$$\therefore$$ $${t \over h} = {{4t - 20} \over {5k}} = {9 \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {t \over h} = {9 \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$ and $${t \over h} = {{4t - 20} \over {5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow t = {{9h} \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$ and $$t = {{20h} \over {4h - 5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{9h} \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}} = {{20h} \over {4h - 5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow h\{ 20({h^2} + {k^2}) - 36h + 45k\} = 0$$

$$x = 0$$ or $$[20({x^2} + {y^2}) - 36x + 45y] = 0$$

questions.questionText: If $$|w| = 2$$, then the set of points $$z = w - {1 \over w}$$ is contained in or equal to the set of points z satisfying
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
To solve this, we need to first express $$w$$ in the polar form since we know the modulus (or absolute value) of $$w$$.

$$w = |w|e^{iθ} = 2e^{iθ}$$

where $$θ$$ is the argument of $$w$$.

Substitute this into the expression for $$z$$:

$$z = w - {1 \over w} = 2e^{iθ} - {1 \over 2e^{iθ}}$$

By applying Euler's formula, which states that $$e^{iθ} = cos(θ) + isin(θ)$$, the expression becomes :

$$z = 2[cos(θ) + isin(θ)] - {1 \over 2}[cos(θ) - isin(θ)]$$

Simplify the expression :

$$z = [2cos(θ) - {1 \over 2}cos(θ)] + i[2sin(θ) + {1 \over 2}sin(θ)]$$

$$z = {3 \over 2}cos(θ) + {5 \over 2}isin(θ)$$

The modulus of $z$ can be found using the formula

$|z| = \sqrt{(Re(z))^2 + (Im(z))^2}$, where $Re(z)$ and $Im(z)$ are the real and imaginary parts of $z$, respectively.

So,

$|z| = \sqrt{(\frac{3}{2} \cos(θ))^2 + (\frac{5}{2} \sin(θ))^2} = \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} \cos^2(θ)) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

We know that $\sin^2(θ) + \cos^2(θ) = 1$ (based on the Pythagorean trigonometric identity), so let's replace $\cos^2(θ)$ with $1 - \sin^2(θ)$ in the equation above.

$|z| = \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} (1 - \sin^2(θ))) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

$= \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} - \frac{9}{4} \sin^2(θ)) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

$= \sqrt{\frac{9}{4} + \frac{16}{4} \sin^2(θ)}$

$= \sqrt{\frac{9 + 16 \sin^2(θ)}{4}}$

$ = \frac{1}{2} \sqrt{9 + 16 \sin^2(θ)}$

Now, $\sin^2(θ)$ is always less than or equal to 1, so $16 \sin^2(θ)$ is always less than or equal to 16.

So, the maximum value of $|z|$ is when $\sin^2(θ) = 1$, which makes $|z|$ equal to $\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{9 + 16} = \frac{1}{2} \sqrt{25} = \frac{5}{2}$.

So the set of points $z$ satisfying $|z| ≤ 3$ (Option D) includes all points $z$, and thus it is the correct choice.

questions.questionText: The smallest positive integral value of n such that $${\left[ {{{1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} + i\cos {\pi \over 8}} \over {1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} - i\cos {\pi \over 8}}}} \right]^n}$$ is purely imaginary, is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$${\left[ {{{1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} + i\cos {\pi \over 8}} \over {1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} - i\cos {\pi \over 8}}}} \right]^n} = {\left[ {{{1 + \cos \alpha + i\sin \alpha } \over {1 + \cos \alpha - i\sin \alpha }}} \right]^n}$$ [$$\because$$ $$\alpha = {\pi \over 2} - {\pi \over 8}$$]

$$ = {\left[ {{{2{{\cos }^2}{\alpha \over 2} + 2i\sin {\alpha \over 2}\cos {\alpha \over 2}} \over {2{{\cos }^2}{\alpha \over 2} - 2i\sin {\alpha \over 2}\cos {\alpha \over 2}}}} \right]^n}$$

$$ = \left[ {{{\cos {\alpha \over 2} + i\sin {\alpha \over 2}} \over {\cos {\alpha \over 2} - i\sin {\alpha \over 2}}}} \right]_ - ^n = {\left( {{e^{2t{\alpha \over 2}}}} \right)^n} = {e^{in\,\alpha }}$$

$$ = {e^{in\left( {{{3\pi } \over 8}} \right)}} = \cos {{3n\pi } \over 8} + i\sin {{3n\pi } \over 8}$$

For n = 4 we get imaginary part.

questions.questionText: $$\left( {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right)\tan x = y{\sec ^2}x + \sin x$$, find general solution
options: $$y = \tan x(\log |{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} x - \cot x| + \cos x + c)$$
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given that

$$\left( {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right)\tan x = y{\sec ^2}x + \sin x$$

$$\tan \left( {{{dy} \over {dx}}} \right) - y{\sec ^2}x = \sin x$$

$${{dy} \over {dx}} + \left( {{{ - {{\sec }^2}x} \over {\tan x}}} \right)y = {{\sin x} \over {\tan x}}$$

Here, $$P = - {{{{\sec }^2}x} \over {\tan x}}$$ and $$Q = \cos x$$

$$IF = {e^{\int {P\,dx} }} = {e^{\int {{{ - {{\sec }^2}x} \over {\tan x}}dx} }}$$

$$IF = {e^{ - \log (\tan x)}} = {e^{\log \cot x}} = \cot x$$

Now, $$y \times IF = \int {(Q \times IF)dx + C} $$

$$y\,.\,\cot x = \int {\cos x\,.\,\cot x + C} $$

$$y\,.\,\cot x = \int {{{1 - {{\sin }^2}x} \over {\sin x}} + C} $$

$$y\,.\,\cot x = \log |{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} \,x - \cot x| + \cos x + C$$
